summary: See clues file for full details. 4 tossups, 3 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 4 tossups and 3 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: This sculptor's statue of a shrouded woman resting her cheek on her right hand was inspired by Japanese Buddhist art introduced to him by John La Farge.
examples: This sculptor's statue of a shrouded woman resting her cheek on her right hand was inspired by Japanese Buddhist art introduced to him by John La Farge.

clues.clue: African-American model Hettie Anderson posed for a winged statue of Victory by this sculptor.
examples: African-American model Hettie Anderson posed for a winged statue of Victory by this sculptor.

clues.clue: Robert Treat Paine created the "cubical pantograph" while working at this sculptor's Cornish Art Colony.
examples: Robert Treat Paine created the "cubical pantograph" while working at this sculptor's Cornish Art Colony.

clues.clue: An angel holds a plaque reading "Amor Caritas" in a motif used by this sculptor, who (*) Stanford White commissioned to design a statue of Diana for Madison Square Garden.
examples: An angel holds a plaque reading "Amor Caritas" in a motif used by this sculptor, who (*) Stanford White commissioned to design a statue of Diana for Madison Square Garden.

clues.clue: At Henry Hobson Richardson's suggestion, this artist created a bronze sculpture inscribed with "he relinquished everything to serve the Republic," which depicts soldiers with rifles accompanying a colonel on horseback.
examples: At Henry Hobson Richardson's suggestion, this artist created a bronze sculpture inscribed with "he relinquished everything to serve the Republic," which depicts soldiers with rifles accompanying a colonel on horseback.

clues.clue: While in Rome, this artist accepted a commission to create a portrait of Eva Rohr as Marguerite from Gounod's Faust.
examples: While in Rome, this artist accepted a commission to create a portrait of Eva Rohr as Marguerite from Gounod's Faust.

clues.clue: This artist's brother depicted Ceres carrying a sickle and a sheaf of wheat, and Thales holding a thunderbolt, in his group The Progress of Railroading.
examples: This artist's brother depicted Ceres carrying a sickle and a sheaf of wheat, and Thales holding a thunderbolt, in his group The Progress of Railroading.

clues.clue: He created a standalone artwork of his favored recurring motif consisting of an angel with raised wings wearing a belt and a crown of passion flowers.
examples: He created a standalone artwork of his favored recurring motif consisting of an angel with raised wings wearing a belt and a crown of passion flowers.

clues.clue: This artist honored the memory of a woman who committed suicide by ingesting potassium cyanide, by depicting her seated against a granite block, eyes closed and wearing a (*) robe.
examples: This artist honored the memory of a woman who committed suicide by ingesting potassium cyanide, by depicting her seated against a granite block, eyes closed and wearing a (*) robe.

clues.clue: This man's sculpture of a president rising from a chair, about to give a speech, is located in Lincoln Park.
examples: This man's sculpture of a president rising from a chair, about to give a speech, is located in Lincoln Park.

clues.clue: This artist's best known sculpture is a bronze relief in which an angel flies over a man on horseback leading a regiment of black soldiers carrying bayonets.
examples: This artist's best known sculpture is a bronze relief in which an angel flies over a man on horseback leading a regiment of black soldiers carrying bayonets.

clues.clue: Near the end of his life, this artist created a monument for his birthplace, Dublin, honoring Charles Stuart Parnell.
examples: Near the end of his life, this artist created a monument for his birthplace, Dublin, honoring Charles Stuart Parnell.
